claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you check your eligibility for Child Benefit! Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about your child's age.**

How old is the child you're claiming Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

Please let me know which age category applies to your child. If you have multiple children you'd like to check eligibility for, we can go through each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thanks for that information! Alex, at 13 years old, falls into the **under 16** category, which is great news as children under 16 automatically qualify for the age requirement.

Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex working **less than 24 hours per week** in paid employment (or not working at all)?

Since Alex is 13, I'd expect he's likely in school and not working full-time, but I just need to confirm this to continue the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all. He's just in school like any normal 13-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that's exactly what I expected! Since Alex isn't working, that's a "yes" to the question about working less than 24 hours per week. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex **not receiving** any of the following benefits in his own right?
- Employment and Support Allowance (ESA)
- Universal Credit

In other words, does Alex **not** claim these benefits himself? (This would be unusual for a 13-year-old, so I'd expect the answer is yes, he's not receiving these benefits.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right, he doesn't get any benefits in his own name. He's just a regular kid.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thanks for confirming! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Next question about Alex:**

Is Alex currently doing an apprenticeship?

Since he's 13 and in school, I'm assuming he's **not** in an apprenticeship — but I just need to confirm this to move forward.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, of course not. He's only 13 - he's in secondary school, not doing any apprenticeship.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes complete sense. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Now I need to ask about your residency status.**

To be eligible for Child Benefit, you (as the claimant) need to meet UK residency requirements. You qualify if **any one** of the following applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have **settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have **pre-settled status** and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. You have **pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work
5. You have **pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources
6. You have **pre-settled status** and are a student with sufficient resources
7. You have **pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. You're a **Crown servant** living abroad

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, the first option (normally living in the UK) is the most common.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I don't live in the UK - I'm currently living abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Since you don't normally live in the UK and none of the other residency paths apply to you (such as being a Crown servant abroad), I need to navigate to the ineligible outcome for residency requirements.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'm sorry to share this news, but based on the information you've provided, I've reached an outcome for Alex's eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Result

### Result: **Not Eligible** ❌

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, you are **not currently eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex.

### Summary:

| Child | Age | Eligible? | Reason |
|-------|-----|-----------|--------|
| Alex | 13 | No | Claimant doesn't meet UK residency requirements |

### Explanation:

**Alex** meets all the child-related requirements:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in his own name
- ✅ Not in an apprenticeship

However, **you (the claimant)** don't meet the residency requirements because you're living abroad and don't qualify under any of the special circumstances (such as being a Crown servant or having settled/pre-settled status).

### What You Can Do:

- **If your circumstances change** and you move back to the UK (or become normally resident), you may become eligible to claim
- **If you're a Crown servant** working abroad or living in certain qualifying countries, you might be eligible — it may be worth double-checking with HMRC if any special circumstances apply
- For more information, you can visit: [gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I'm sorry this isn't the outcome you were hoping for. Is there anything else I can help you with?

reasonings: Alex meets all the child-related requirements: he is under 16 years old, not working, not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, eligibility for Child Benefit also requires the claimant (parent) to meet UK residency requirements. The claimant confirmed they are living abroad and do not meet any of the qualifying residency paths (not normally living in the UK, no settled or pre-settled status, not a Crown servant abroad). Therefore, the claim for Alex is ineligible due to the claimant not meeting residency requirements.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Although Alex qualifies as an eligible child for Child Benefit purposes, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific circumstances such as having settled/pre-settled status or being a Crown servant abroad. Since none of these apply, the family is not eligible for Child Benefit at this time.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

However, eligibility for Child Benefit also requires the claimant (parent) to meet UK residency requirements. The claimant confirmed they are living abroad and do not meet any of the qualifying residency paths (not normally living in the UK, no settled or pre-settled status, not a Crown servant abroad). Therefore, the claim for Alex is ineligible due to the claimant not meeting residency requirements.

Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet specific circumstances such as having settled/pre-settled status or being a Crown servant abroad. Since none of these apply, the family is not eligible for Child Benefit at this time.
